data.title: Silver heads for sixth straight annual deficit with drawdown raising squeeze risk
data.description:
The silver market faces a ‌sixth consecutive year of structural deficit, with 762M oz drawn from inventories since 2021, raising the risk of a renewed liquidity squeeze despite weaker demand expectations, the Silver Institute and consultancy Metals Focus said Wednesday.

The global silver market deficit is expected to widen to 46.3M oz ​in 2026 from 40.3M oz ​in 2025, even as ⁠total demand falls 2% due to weaker industrial and jewelry consumption, partly offset by stronger coin and bar demand, the research showed.

Liquidity has improved since last October's squeeze, as metal flowed back from the U.S., silver-backed ​exchange-traded products saw outflows, and Indian demand eased, "but risks of another liquidity squeeze this year remain," said Philip Newman, managing director at Metals Focus, which prepared the research for the ​Silver Institute.

Conditions for a silver squeeze will be created again, requiring further outflows from the U.S., if the price becomes more volatile and Indian demand gets active, especially coupled with inflows to ETPs storing their metal in London, Newman said.

Gold futures hovered ~$4,800/oz and silver at nearly $80/oz on Wednesday, as hopes for a fresh round of talks between the U.S. and Iran eased inflation concerns tied to higher energy prices.

"A softer dollar has supported the recovery; however, a sustained breakout likely depends on further constructive developments in the Middle East," Saxo Bank analysts said in a note.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for April delivery finished down 0.5% to $4,800.00/oz, while front-month Comex April silver (XAGUSD:CUR) ticked up 0.1% to $79.491/oz.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (DUST), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Goldstorm Metals upsizes private placement to $7 million
data.description:
• Goldstorm Metals (GSTM:CA) (FSE: B2U) on Wednesday said it has increased the size of its non-brokered private placement to up to $7 million from $4 million, citing strong investor demand.
  • The offering includes multiple unit types priced between $0.20 and $0.31, each consisting of common shares and warrants with an exercise price of $0.30 and a 36-month term.
  • Proceeds from certain units will be used for exploration spending qualifying under Canadian tax rules, while remaining funds will be used for general corporate purposes.
  • The offering is expected to close around the week of April 30, subject to regulatory approvals.
  • NEM +0.23% after hours to $113.3.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Lockheed Martin Space secures $850M U.S. Navy contract modification
data.description:
• Lockheed Martin Space, a business area of Lockheed Martin Corporation (LMT), won an $850.41M contract modification for the TRIDENT II (D5) Life Extension 2 program, covering advanced design and development work.
  • The work is expected to be completed by September 30, 2030.
  • FY2026 U.S. Navy weapons procurement funds of  $850.41M will be obligated at the time of award, with no funds expiring in the current fiscal year.
  • The Strategic Systems Programs is the contracting activity.

data.title: Stocks to watch after market on Wednesday: CDNA, JBHT, SLG, QDEL
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages advanced on Wednesday, with the S&P 500 (SP500) and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) ending at an all-time high as investors priced in good news on the U.S.-Iran conflict front while parsing more bank and financial earnings.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) closed at the record of 7,022.93 points, ending +0.8%.

Here are the four stocks to watch on Wednesday after market:

CareDx (CDNA) surged over 8% in extended trading Wednesday after announcing it will divest its Lab Products business to EuroBio Scientific for $170 million in cash. The precision medicine company also disclosed preliminary first-quarter revenue of approximately $118 million, well ahead of the $102.49 million Wall Street consensus and representing 39% year-over-year growth.

J.B. Hunt Transport Services (JBHT) gained 1.3% in postmarket trading after the logistics company topped analyst expectations with its fiscal first-quarter results. The company reported GAAP EPS of $1.49, beating the $1.46 consensus estimate, while operating revenue rose 5% year-over-year to $3.06 billion.

SL Green Realty Corp. (SLG) sank 4.0% in after-hours trading after Manhattan’s largest office landlord posted first-quarter FFO per share of $0.84, missing the $1.08 consensus estimate. Revenue of $253.1 million did top the $242.4 million expectation, and the company signed 51 Manhattan office leases covering 929,264 square feet during the quarter, its highest first-quarter volume on record.

QuidelOrtho (QDEL) tumbled approximately 11% following the market close after issuing preliminary first-quarter revenue guidance with a midpoint of $617.5 million, well below the $679.59 million consensus. The diagnostics company attributed the shortfall to a weaker respiratory season, with U.S. influenza-like illness visits down roughly 30% year-over-year, along with proposed reimbursement changes in China and delayed orders in EMEA due to the Middle East conflict.

data.title: Google's SpaceX stake could deliver $100 billion payday post-IPO -- report
data.description:
Thanks to a 6.11% stake in SpaceX (SPACE) Google LLC, a wholly owned subsidiary of Alphabet (GOOG), could make at least $100B from its investment once the space exploration company goes public.

According to a regulatory filing in Alaska, Google’s initial investment has been diluted to ~5% due to the dilution impact of the SpaceX/xAI merger, but it is still worth billions of dollars should the valuation reach a record-setting $2T valuation.  

While the SpaceX IPO stands to make billionaires out of early investors, including SpaceX President Gwynne Shotwell, Fidelity Investment’s Contrafund, Founders Fund, and even some original SpaceX employees, CEO Elon Musk could become history’s first trillionaire with his 40%+ holding in the company.

With so many individuals at the company poised to see their financial futures change dramatically, Bloomberg raises the possibility of a brain drain in the wake of the IPO.

“One of the biggest questions I have for this is what happens to middle management, even though SpaceX is a very lean company, and some of the upper management that after the IPO will not need to do anything to support themselves,” PitchBook’s Franco Granda said to Bloomberg.

“The investors who got in at 2021 will have life-changing returns, if not career-defining,” Granda added.

SpaceX submitted a confidential filing to go public, aiming for a June offering. The IPO is expected to raise at least $75B, making it the largest initial public offering on record.

The company is reportedly weighing several alternative IPO structures, including a dual-class share structure that would give insiders—including Musk—extra voting power, a tiered lock-up period that would release shares every 20 to 30 days to avert mass selling of shares after the traditional 180-day lock-up period, and a higher-than-usual retail allocation of shares of 30%.

data.title: Thinkific CEO to directly oversee product and tech teams to speed AI development
data.description:
• Thinkific Labs (THNC:CA) on Wednesday said its Chief Executive Greg Smith will take direct supervision of its product and technology teams as part of a push to accelerate AI-driven innovation.
  • The company said the change is intended to streamline decision-making and shorten product development timelines, with Smith overseeing execution of core R&D strategy while existing vice presidents continue leading operational functions.
  • Thinkific also said Ryan Donovan will be leaving the company to pursue other opportunities.
  • THNCF closed -0.96% at $1.03.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Allegiant Travel Company, Sun Country Airlines win DOT approval for merger step
data.description:
• Allegiant Travel Company (ALGT) and Sun Country Airlines (SNCY) on Wednesday said that the U.S. Department of Transportation has approved an interim exemption allowing their airlines to operate separately under common ownership following closing.
  • The approval marks a key regulatory milestone for Allegiant’s proposed acquisition of Sun Country and satisfies the final regulatory condition tied to the deal.
  • The companies said they now expect the transaction to close as early as May 13, 2026, subject to shareholder approvals scheduled for May 8 and other customary conditions.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Mineral Road Discovery appoints new CFO
data.description:
• Mineral Road Discovery (CRSTF) has appointed Alex Helmel as chief financial officer effective April 14, 2026.
  • Helmel is an independent consultant with experience in early-stage ventures and Canadian capital markets.

data.title: Large-cap real estate stocks with A grade EPS revisions as earnings season begins
data.description:
As Q1 2026 earnings season gets underway, the real estate sector is entering reporting season on a relative high, as investors sought defensive, income-generating exposure amid broader market volatility.

This list highlights large cap real estate stocks screened for Seeking Alpha EPS revision Quant Grades of A- or better, offering investors a look at companies in the sector demonstrating strong earnings momentum.

The top tier of the list features three companies earning the highest A+ grade: Equinix, Inc. (EQIX), a Data Center REIT with a market cap of $104.28 billion; W. P. Carey Inc. (WPC), a Diversified REIT valued at $15.70 billion; and Weyerhaeuser Company (WY), a Timber REIT with a $17.87 billion market cap. This diversity across REIT subcategories demonstrates that strong EPS revisions span multiple real estate segments.

The remaining stocks on the list include several Retail and Health Care REITs. Regency Centers Corporation (REG) and Simon Property Group, Inc. (SPG) represent the Retail REIT space with A grades, while Ventas, Inc. (VTR) earns an A grade in the Health Care REIT category. Notably, several stocks combine high EPS revision grades with favorable Quant ratings, including Jones Lang LaSalle Incorporated (JLL) and W. P. Carey Inc. (WPC), both carrying Strong Buy ratings.

  1. Equinix, Inc. (EQIX): EPS revision grade A+

  2. W. P. Carey Inc. (WPC): EPS revision grade A+

  3. Weyerhaeuser Company (WY): EPS revision grade A+

  4. Regency Centers Corporation (REG): EPS revision grade A

  5. Simon Property Group, Inc. (SPG): EPS revision grade A

  6. Ventas, Inc. (VTR): EPS revision grade A

  7. Jones Lang LaSalle Incorporated (JLL): EPS revision grade A-

  8. Kimco Realty Corporation (KIM): EPS revision grade A-

  9. Welltower Inc. (WELL): EPS revision grade A-

Real Estate ETFs: (VNQ), (XLRE), (IYR), (USRT), and (HOMZ)

data.title: Nine Mile Metals launches $1M private placement for exploration
data.description:
• Nine Mile Metals (VMSXF) plans a non-brokered private placement to raise up to ~$1M.
  • The offering includes up to 7.14M units priced at $0.14 each.
  • Each unit consists of one flow-through common share and one warrant.
  • Warrants are exercisable at $0.30 for two years, with potential early expiry if share price targets are met.
  • The offering is expected to close around April 20, 2026.
  • Proceeds will fund exploration at the Bathurst Mining Camp critical minerals projects.
